mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate earnings and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicate supply constraints that erode profit margins and force negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4 percent triggers immediate market repricing of future rate hikes, causing negative short-term equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with increasing retail sales indicate strengthening household balance sheets and consumer confidence, generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent increases in HOUST and RSAFS result in negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during monetary tightening cycles signal immediate economic slowdown fears that trigger rapid equity liquidation over one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances, invalidating the slowdown fear mechanism.
